rookie.title: Murder Express
rookie.flavor: The Orient Express cuts through the snowy Carpathian Mountains when a scream pierces the night. In the morning, a passenger is found dead in their compartment, and the train is snowbound with nowhere to run.
rookie.emptyState: All aboard for murder. Final destination: justice.

items.name: Viktor Petrova
items.detail: Romanian businessman with connections to underground networks.

items.name: Countess Elena
items.detail: Austrian nobility fleeing political troubles in Vienna.

items.name: Dr. Heinrich
items.detail: German physician traveling to Constantinople on mysterious business.

items.name: Marcel Dubois
items.detail: French art dealer with a reputation for acquiring questionable pieces.

items.name: Letter Opener
items.detail: Ornate silver blade found beneath the victim's berth.

items.name: Poison Vial
items.detail: Empty glass container hidden in a medical bag.

items.name: Heavy Candlestick
items.detail: Brass fixture from the dining car with suspicious stains.

items.name: Silk Scarf
items.detail: Expensive accessory that could have been used for strangulation.

items.name: Victim's Compartment
items.detail: Private sleeper car where the body was discovered.

items.name: Dining Car
items.detail: Scene of the victim's final meal and heated conversations.

items.name: Observation Deck
items.detail: Rear platform where passengers took evening air.

items.name: Baggage Car
items.detail: Storage area accessible only to staff and certain passengers.

clues.heading: Locked Door Mystery
clues.text: The victim's compartment door was locked from the inside, but the window latch shows signs of tampering.
clues.alone: The compartment was secured from within, suggesting an inside job or specialized knowledge.

clues.heading: Midnight Encounter
clues.text: A passenger reports seeing Viktor Petrova arguing intensely with the victim near the baggage car around midnight. The conversation appeared to involve money and threats.
clues.alone: Viktor was seen in a heated confrontation with the victim late at night.

clues.heading: Medical Analysis
clues.text: The victim shows signs of strychnine poisoning, a substance commonly found in medical supplies. Several passengers had access to the poison through various means.
clues.alone: Death was caused by strychnine, pointing to someone with medical knowledge or connections.

clues.heading: Technical Expertise
clues.text: Dr. Heinrich's background includes not only medicine but also engineering training from his military service. His compartment contains tools that could easily manipulate the window mechanism.
clues.alone: The doctor possesses both medical knowledge and mechanical skills from his past.

clues.heading: Alibi Confirmation
clues.text: The conductor confirms Viktor Petrova was in the dining car playing cards with other passengers from 1 AM until dawn. Multiple witnesses verify he never left the game.
clues.alone: Viktor has a solid alibi from multiple sources for the critical time period.

clues.heading: Poison Source
clues.text: Strychnine residue is found in Dr. Heinrich's medical bag, along with a syringe that matches the injection mark on the victim. His medical supplies were recently accessed. Countess Elena was.
clues.alone: Countess Elena and Marcel Dubois are cleared by independent witnesses.

rookie.epilogue: Dr. Heinrich killed to protect his daughter's reputation, as the victim possessed evidence of her involvement in a Berlin scandal that would have destroyed her marriage prospects. He used his medical knowledge to administer the poison and his engineering background to create the locked room illusion. Though his methods were calculated, his motive was purely paternal love, making him a sympathetic killer driven by desperation rather than malice.

clues.heading: Locked Door Mystery
clues.text: The victim's compartment door was locked from the inside, but the window latch shows signs of tampering.
clues.alone: The compartment was secured from within, suggesting an inside job or specialized knowledge.

clues.heading: Conductor's Report
clues.text: The conductor swears he saw Marcel Dubois leaving the victim's compartment at 2 AM, looking disheveled and nervous. He appeared to be concealing something in his coat pocket..
clues.alone: Marcel Dubois is a red herring. Petrova and Elena have solid alibis.

clues.heading: Hidden Letters
clues.text: A cache of letters found in Anna Schmidt's luggage reveals the victim was threatening to expose her father's war crimes unless she provided information about her employer's family..
clues.alone: Anna was being blackmailed about her father's dark past and forced into espionage. Protection of family is her motive.

clues.heading: Technical Knowledge
clues.text: Anna Schmidt's personal effects include mechanical drawings and railway blueprints - remnants from her previous job at a German engineering firm. She understands train construction better than she admits.
clues.alone: The quiet governess has hidden technical expertise from her industrial background.

clues.heading: Mistaken Identity
clues.text: Upon closer inspection, several passengers note that in the dim lighting, Marcel's dark coat and Anna's masculine travel attire could easily be confused. Dr. Heinrich was treating a.
clues.alone: The witness identification may have been compromised by poor lighting and similar clothing.

clues.heading: Modified Weapon
clues.text: Anna's fountain pen has been expertly modified with a hollow needle tip and internal reservoir for liquid injection.
clues.alone: The governess created a sophisticated poison delivery system using her technical skills.

detective.epilogue: Anna Schmidt killed to protect her father from exposure of his war crimes, which would have destroyed what remained of his reputation and her family's name. She used her hidden engineering skills to modify a fountain pen into a poison delivery device and her knowledge of train mechanics to create a locked room mystery. Though she appeared to be a quiet governess, Anna was actually a brilliant engineer driven to murder by filial loyalty and the victim's relentless blackmail. Her technical expertise and desperate love for her father made her both the perfect killer and the most tragic figure on the train.

items.detail: Communications specialist who could intercept and send messages.

items.name: Mechanical Engineer
items.detail: Technical expert responsible for train maintenance and operation.

items.name: Security Guard
items.detail: Armed protection officer with authority to access restricted areas.

clues.heading: Complex Entry Method
clues.text: The victim's compartment was locked from inside, but analysis reveals the killer accessed through the service corridor beneath, using specialized knowledge of the train's hidden maintenance passages.
clues.alone: The murder required intimate knowledge of the train's hidden infrastructure and staff cooperation.

clues.heading: Chef's Testimony
clues.text: The dining car chef insists he saw Dr. Heinrich disposing of medical equipment in the kitchen waste around 3 AM. Viktor Petrova, Countess Elena, and Marcel Dubois were.
clues.alone: Petrova, Elena, Dubois, and Kozlov are all cleared by dining car witnesses.

clues.heading: Blackmail Network
clues.text: Documents reveal the victim was systematically blackmailing multiple passengers, including Anna Schmidt over her father's war crimes. The letters reveal she acted out of protection for her family.
clues.alone: Anna was trapped in a blackmail scheme that threatened to destroy her future prospects. Protection of family is her motive.

clues.heading: Staff Conspiracy
clues.text: The mechanical engineer's duty logs show unauthorized access to the service corridors during the murder timeframe, with entries that were hastily altered afterward.
clues.alone: Railway staff provided crucial assistance in executing the impossible murder method.

clues.heading: Kitchen Confusion
clues.text: The chef's night vision was severely impaired by kitchen steam and dim lighting, and he admits Dr.
clues.alone: The witness identification was compromised by poor visibility and physical similarities between suspects.

clues.heading: Military Precision
clues.text: Anna Schmidt's fountain pen modification shows military-grade engineering consistent with techniques taught to covert operatives, and the mechanical engineer's toolkit contains the exact specialized tools needed for such.
clues.alone: The weapon modification required both covert training and professional engineering assistance.

inspector.epilogue: Anna Schmidt killed to protect her father from exposure of his war crimes, which would have meant his execution and her family's complete ruin. She enlisted the help of the mechanical engineer, who had his own reasons to hate the victim's extortion network that preyed on railway workers. Together, they created an elaborate locked room murder using Anna's covert operations training and the engineer's intimate knowledge of the train's hidden passages. Anna's desperate love for her father and the engineer's solidarity with a fellow victim of blackmail forged an unlikely alliance that nearly created the perfect crime, demonstrating how ordinary people can be driven to extraordinary measures when cornered by systematic cruelty.
